prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) for neutral pyridine; headline value is the largest electrophilic Fukui index.
• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density-fitting (PySCF backend).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied pyridine.xyz; single-point only (no re-optimization) → DFT//input geometry.
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet).
• Software: chemkit “fukui-reactivity” skill (PySCF v2.x under the hood).
• Convergence: SCF self-consistency achieved; integrity.status = "ok".
• Warnings surfaced verbatim (see below) — none suppressed.

Key results (dimensionless):
• Most electrophilic site: atom 1 (N) with f⁺ = 0.48422572  ← headline number reported above.
• Most nucleophilic site: atom 4 (C) with f⁻ = 0.1182626.

Reproducibility
• Command executed (from result JSON):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp07aqzubc/live.json
• Output JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp07aqzubc/live.json
• Live engine log (.out) path was announced when the run started; it can be inspected or tailed for full SCF/optimizer details.

Caveats
The integrity checker flags charge-conservation warnings (sum(f⁺) and sum(f⁻) each exactly 1.0000) that may indicate subtle convergence sensitivity of the N ± 1 states; values should be interpreted with typical qualitative caution for Fukui analyses.
